# Bearing — Full Context

> AI model recommendation tool with transparent, multi-factor scoring

## Architecture

Bearing is a Next.js 15 application using the App Router, Neon Postgres, and Claude Haiku for task classification. Models are scored using a pure-function scoring engine with no side effects.

## How recommendations work

1. User describes their AI task in natural language
2. Claude Haiku classifies the task: type, complexity, capabilities needed, pipeline stages
3. User sets priorities by ranking 7 factors (can exclude factors they don't care about)
4. Scoring engine computes weighted scores for all eligible models
5. Results are ranked and displayed with full factor breakdowns
6. For complex tasks, quality and capability weights are automatically boosted

## How comparisons work

1. User selects any two models from the 29 available
2. Enters a prompt (optionally with a PDF or CSV attachment)
3. Both models receive the same prompt via OpenRouter API or direct provider APIs
4. Responses displayed side-by-side for preference voting
5. Preference data is stored for community scoring research

## Scoring methodology

Weights are computed from user priority ranking (rank-based with default blending):
- Top priority: ~28-30% weight
- Bottom priority: ~4-5% weight
- Complexity boost: complex tasks multiply quality weight by 1.5x, capability by 1.3x
- Factor exclusion: users can toggle off factors, remaining weights renormalise

## API providers

- OpenRouter: 26 models via unified API
- GreenPT: 2 models (green-l, green-r) via direct API
- Mistral: 1 model (Pixtral Large / Mistral OCR) via direct API
- Claude Haiku: classification and reasoning (via Anthropic SDK)

## Data model

- Tasks: classification results, priority order, excluded factors
- Recommendations: ranked model lists with factor scores
- Selections: which model the user chose
- Comparisons: side-by-side prompt results with preference votes
- Outcomes: user feedback on model performance

## Open data

All comparison outcomes and preference data are published openly at /data for community research.

## Source

https://github.com/dataforaction-tom/bearing
